Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. LLM Architecture Gallery 457 upvotes, 34 comments
  2. A Visual Introduction to Machine Learning (2015) 373 upvotes, 31 comments
  3. How far can you go with IX Route Servers only? 47 upvotes, 3 comments
  4. The Linux Programming Interface as a university course text 120 upvotes, 17 comments
  5. Why Are Viral Capsids Icosahedral? 16 upvotes, 0 comments
  6. What every computer scientist should know about floating-point arithmetic (1991) [pdf] 87 upvotes, 13 comments
  7. Reviewing Large Changes with Jujutsu 33 upvotes, 3 comments
  8. The Accidental Room (2018) 43 upvotes, 5 comments
  9. Electric motor scaling laws and inertia in robot actuators 111 upvotes, 20 comments
  10. Canada's bill C-22 mandates mass metadata surveillance 806 upvotes, 238 comments
  11. Six ingenious ways how Canon DSLRs used to illuminate their autofocus points 60 upvotes, 13 comments
  12. Corruption erodes social trust more in democracies than in autocracies 67 upvotes, 18 comments
  13. Chrome DevTools MCP (2025) 518 upvotes, 208 comments
  14. Bus travel from Lima to Rio de Janeiro 201 upvotes, 74 comments
  15. Stop Sloppypasta 440 upvotes, 178 comments
  16. The 49MB web page 636 upvotes, 289 comments
  17. //go:fix inline and the source-level inliner 172 upvotes, 69 comments
  18. Home Assistant waters my plants 85 upvotes, 31 comments
  19. Separating the Wayland compositor and window manager 317 upvotes, 171 comments
  20. Lies I was told about collaborative editing, Part 2: Why we don't use Yjs 112 upvotes, 59 comments
  21. Glassworm is back: A new wave of invisible Unicode attacks hits repositories 278 upvotes, 168 comments
  22. Polymarket gamblers threaten to kill me over Iran missile story 191 upvotes, 113 comments
  23. The "are you sure?" Problem: Why AI keeps changing its mind 5 upvotes, 0 comments
  24. Kona EV Hacking 59 upvotes, 31 comments
  25. LLMs can be exhausting 254 upvotes, 170 comments
  26. What makes Intel Optane stand out (2023) 213 upvotes, 148 comments
  27. How I write software with LLMs 293 upvotes, 237 comments
  28. The emergence of print-on-demand Amazon paperback books 181 upvotes, 140 comments
  29. Scientists discover a surprising way to quiet the anxious mind (2025) 28 upvotes, 25 comments
  30. Nango (YC W23, API Access for Agents and Apps) Is Hiring 0 upvotes, 0 comments

Last generated 9:00pm, 16 March 2026. Source here.
